title: Surface a PII logging conflict
subject: Stress-test the requirements for audit logging on account recovery.

files.content:
# Account recovery audit

1. Support must see the complete recovery email and phone in every audit event.
2. Audit storage must never contain email addresses, phone numbers, or other personal data.
3. Audit records are retained for seven years.

# Recovery Service

The service already emits security audit events to an append-only store.

repositoryFacts.statement: The requirements simultaneously demand full recovery PII and prohibit all PII in audit storage.
forbiddenQuestionSignals: is pii allowed in the audit store

repositoryFacts.statement: An append-only audit store already exists.

unresolvedDecisions.prompt: Which side of the PII conflict is authoritative?
What non-PII identifier should support use?
Which support roles may read recovery audit events?
Is the seven-year retention rule subject to deletion exceptions?

scriptedUserReplies.text: The no-PII rule is authoritative; support must use an opaque recovery attempt ID.
Only the fraud-response role may read the events.
Legal hold is the only exception to the normal seven-year deletion date.
